Summarize in exactly one sentence: Quantum entanglement is a physical phenomenon that occurs when pairs or groups of particles are generated, interact, or share spatial proximity in a way such that the quantum state of each particle cannot be described independently of the state of the others, even when the particles are separated by a large distance. This counterintuitive property was famously called spooky action at a distance by Einstein. It is a fundamental aspect of quantum mechanics and has applications in quantum computing, quantum cryptography, and quantum teleportation.
